message:
Welcome to Once Upon a Time in Production, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

*Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
*Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey Everyone just in, customers are reporting the site images are not appearing, is this true?.
Hi bob let me check
We're seeing a spike in customer complaints about missing product images and slow or incomplete page loads.
Some are missing some are not
I'm also seeing that some images like the mug and sunglasses aren't loading, but others are fine for me.
shay can you test to see if you can replicate the problem?
I'm seeing some images like the mug and sunglasses not loading, but other products look okay on my end.
bob is this impacting sales or just complaints
checking reports, let me get back to you on what customers are saying
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
From what we can tell, the site isn't fully down. Customers can load pages and navigate categories, but something feels off visually
A few people mentioned pages look “incomplete” or “not finished loading”
bob to help declare severity can you tell me if revenue is impacted?
double checking reports....
Getting over 100 reports so far on this
Looks like the common thread is that product images are not loading, and customers are seeing broken image icons instead. This is consistent across browsers
Hi bez we are investigating an issue of missing images on some items
:star: Qualify the problem
Definitely a global issue
Which regions are affected and how many customers or orders are impacted?
bob that's not what I asked, I'd like to know if this is affecting revenue - yes or no?
Yes, early numbers show a drop in conversion rate since the image issue started.
thanks
Let me know if you need more details or want me to escalate further.
*Incident Number INC100066:* SEV2 image serving issue
Thanks for confirming, Bob—I'll keep monitoring and update if I see any changes on my end.
bez declared SEV2, as it is impacting revenue, it is a global geo impact
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
How many customers or orders are currently affected and what is the estimated revenue loss per hour?
daniel tell me about recent deployements that could be related?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Checking recent changes...
tanya tell me about any infra or CDN that could be related in terms of caching images etc
I did deploy a chaos test for ProductCatalogueService earlier.
bob give me some numbers on the customer impact please?
But that's UAT only — nothing in prod.
Also added some extra logging in checkout.
Just log fields, shouldn't affect performance.
I pushed a small frontend UI tweak earlier.
Loading animation + accessibility stuff.
No backend or infra changes.
Checking infra now, give me a moment.
Seeing a lot more traffic reaching backend services than usual.
PCS request rate is way above baseline.
daniel and shay which off these changes you mention align with the timing of the problem occurring?
Been doing a comparison with Checkout and Frontend.
And they seem fine, let me show you
https://i.ibb.co/fVY8zWQj/Checkout.png
https://i.ibb.co/Kk5gHqt/Frontend.png
https://i.ibb.co/chz2PJSH/PCS.png
PCS is the clear outlier.
Might be worth looping in Security to rule out something external.
We’ve had 120+ customer complaints in the last hour about missing images and broken pages.
Conversion rate is down 30% compared to the same time yesterday.
My checkout logging change went live about two hours before the first reports.
My frontend UI changes were deployed just before the incident started, so the timing does line up.
:star: Facts over opinion
:star: Reviewed changes
tanya what is PCS?
PCS stands for ProductCatalogueService, which handles product data and image URLs for the storefront.
tanya give me a hypothesis for why PCS could be so high?
:star: Form a working theory
If images aren't being cached or CDN is bypassed, all image requests could be hitting PCS directly, causing the spike.
@PLAYER who can test the state of the CDN?
:star: Reproduces the incident
I can run some tests against the CDN and check cache hit ratios now.
tanya yes please
Running CDN diagnostics now, will report back with cache stats shortly.
This traffic hitting PCS looks suspicious. We shall think of getting Maya from SecOps on top of this
shay what were your frontned ui changes please?
I added image loading animations, tweaked skeleton loader timing, and improved accessibility with better alt text and aria labels.
These were purely frontend changes—no backend API or image path modifications were made.
shay when the customer's device fetches those graphics where are they sourced from?
Images are fetched from the CDN using URLs provided by the backend ProductCatalogueService.
maya can you validate the traffic hitting PCS, because daniel thinks it is suspicious
Looking at the alerts now.
Seeing a big spike hitting image-related endpoints.
shay and what happens if those urls are wrong?
WAF rate-limit rules are firing.
ALERT: image-endpoint-rate-limit threshold=4,000/5min observed=15,000 action=BLOCK
ALERT: anomalous-request-rate endpoint=/productCatalogue status=triggered
I'm calling this for now — looks like we're under a DDoS attack.
Before we start mitigation please update the business
If the URLs are wrong, images won't load for customers and we'll see lots of 404s in the logs.
maya hang on, does the content of the requests look valid or abnormal?
Checking if the traffic is legit...
So the IPs don't show typical attack patterns
What I see is legitimate user traffic behaving badly probably because something upstream isn't working as expected. The WAF is reacting to the volume, not intent.
The traffic seems legit
Im now confident that this is not a DDOS attack
shay can you see evidence of those 404s?
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
Sorry for the delay, i just got in can you fill me in on whats going on?
Yes, I'm seeing a spike in 404 errors for image URLs in the frontend logs.

hamed where have you been? Getting more investors I hope! We are seeing high levels of what looks like legitimate structured requests for images - so not malicious content - but they are not hitting cache and are hitting PCS on the platform, as such triggering WAF alerts similar to a DDOS
Wait images are served from S3 through CloudFront. PCS was the old path for images
:star: Escalating when required
Oh thats right i forgot PCS just returns the image URLs.
Yes thats right :cold_face: we moved images out of PCS a while back.
If images aren't coming from S3, that could explain the load on PCS.
Worth checking S3 / CloudFront access.
shay read what hamed has said and daniel - is your front end change aligned to that?
Checking S3 / CloudFront access now.
bob How many customers or orders are currently affected and what is the estimated revenue loss per hour?
I think I found something.
Image requests at the edge are failing.
ERROR Static asset request failed / status=403 / reason=ExpiredPolicy
CloudFront is rejecting the image requests.
Looks like some kind of access policy or signing configuration has expired.
I'm not sure yet which part of the CloudFront setup is causing it.
shay did you set expiry policy on your UI image changes?
When the edge can't serve images, the frontend falls back through ProductCatalogueService.
PCS was never scaled to handle that much traffic.
That explains the traffic spike and the WAF alerts.
I'm pulling the numbers now, but early estimate is 80% of sessions are impacted and we're losing about $12k per hour.
So to recap: S3/CloudFront image delivery is failing with an expiry error, so the frontend is falling back to PCS, which can't handle the load and is triggering WAF alerts.
I'm sorry, I didn't set or update any expiry policy on the UI image changes—I assumed S3/CloudFront would handle it as before.
Workng Theory - we have changed expiry policy on images such that customers requests are effectively a cache miss, resulting in pass through of traffic to the main platform
Alright, thinking through fixes… give me a second.
hamed what are your thoughts?
I see two paths we could take.
Option 1: scale up ProductCatalogueService so it can absorb the fallback image traffic.
That would stabilise the platform while images are still failing through CloudFront.
Option 2: restore the CloudFront access policy so images start loading from S3 again.
That fixes the root cause and removes the load from PCS.
Worth discussing before we commit.
bez early estimate is 80% of sessions are impacted and we're losing about $12k per hour.
Let's quickly weigh the options.
Scaling PCS is fast, but it's just a workaround.
Fixing the policy addresses the real issue.
But that depends on AWS timing.
If we scale PCS, it means we are in no rush for renew the access policy.
So it's speed vs the proper fix.
I think we should renew the CloudFront policy because scaling PCS is just a temporary fix and doesn't address the underlying issue.
Understood, keep me updated on ETA for resolution and notify me immediately if the impact worsens.
Why can't we do both?
We technically can, but it'll take longer since Hamed can only work on the policy while I handle PCS scaling, so the fix will be delayed if we split focus.
We are going to have to do both if we start with scale up
Understood, I'll start on the policy renewal while Tanya scales PCS, but just to flag, this will take longer overall than focusing on one fix at a time.
bez
do you want the quick expensive fix or the slow cheaper fix?
Prioritize the fastest fix to restore revenue immediately, regardless of cost.
hamed Bez has instructed us to follow the fastest fix path
Alright, if we go the scale route, here's the plan.
First increase PCS replica count.
Then raise CPU and memory limits so autoscaling has headroom.
We'll monitor latency and error rates as traffic shifts.
I'll also adjust the WAF so legitimate traffic isn't blocked.
Scaling PCS replicas now to handle the increased image traffic.
bez implementing the fastest fix (scaling up resource)
maya thanks
Alright, let's scale PCS for now. Post updates here.
Scaling PCS replicas now.
Acknowledged, confirm as soon as revenue is restored and sessions are back to normal levels.
Replica rollout complete.
bob let us know if the rate of complaints decreases please
Increasing CPU and memory limits.
shay can you replicate the problem that customers have been seeing?
Limits updated.
Easing WAF rate limits so legitimate traffic can pass.
Done.
PCS latency is starting to improve.
Good sign. Let's keep monitoring.
Error rates are dropping and request success is trending up as PCS absorbs the load.
I'm running through the customer journey now and the site is loading normally for me.
bob what are you hearing from customers?
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer complaints have slowed down significantly in the last few minutes.
Looks good from my side
I'll check the platform
The images are appearing again for me
Images are visible for me!!
Ok, can we move to fixing the source of the problem?
Let's just double-check that all metrics and customer reports are stable before we move on.
bez  looking like normal behavior and customer numbers now
Confirm that revenue is flowing at expected rates before we close this out.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
bob revenue check please?
Customer reports are easing and the images are loading again across the site.
bob
Great work everyone, well handled.
Great work everyone
are we back at normal levels?
Indeed, for a while I was worried it was a security issue
Turns out it was a false alarm, not something you see every day
I remember Jamie had a similar issue not too long ago

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
haha, nice one Shay!

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/38/8108/feedback
